  ],

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go", "symbol": "NewAlertNG"},

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go", "symbol": "NewAlertRuleService"},

  "text": "The grafana/grafana repository contains a comprehensive implementation of both legacy alerting and unified alerting (ngalert) systems, along with migration infrastructure to convert legacy rules to unified format.\n\n**Legacy Alerting Storage:**\nThe legacy alerting notification system is primarily housed in `pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/legacy_storage/` which includes:\n- `config.go`: Contains `AlertmanagerConfigStoreImpl` for managing legacy Alertmanager configurations\n- `persist.go`: Defines the `AMConfigStore` interface for persisting alert configurations\n- `receivers.go`: Implements `ReceiverService` for managing legacy notification channels/receivers\n- `compat.go`: Compatibility layer for converting between legacy and unified alert formats\n\nLegacy settings are read from the configuration in `pkg/setting/setting.go` and `pkg/setting/setting_unified_alerting.go`, with the `readAlertingSettings()` function ensuring legacy alerting cannot be enabled in newer versions.\n\n**Unified Alerting (ngalert) Core Engine:**\nThe unified alerting system is implemented in `pkg/services/ngalert/` with key components:\n- `ngalert.go`: Contains `AlertNG` service that manages all alerting components\n- `models/alert_rule.go`: Defines the `AlertRule` model with support for migrated rules (using `MigratedLabelPrefix` and related constants like `MigratedUseLegacyChannelsLabel`)\n- `store/database.go`: Implements `DBstore` for persisting alert rules and instances to the database\n- `store/alert_rule.go`: Implements the `RuleStore` interface for CRUD operations on alert rules\n- `store/compat.go`: Contains conversion logic between different alert rule formats and notification settings\n\n**Alert Rule Storage Implementations:**\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/store/provisioning_store.go`: Implements the `ProvisioningStore` interface for managing rule provenance\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go`: Full database operations for alert rules including insertion, updates, and queries\n\n**Provisioning Service:**\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go`: `AlertRuleService` handles rule provisioning, validation, and management\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/persist.go`: Defines `RuleStore` and `ProvisioningStore` interfaces\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/contactpoints.go`: Manages contact points which are provisioned notification destinations\n\n**Migration from Datasource-Managed to Grafana-Managed Rules:**\nThe conversion API for Prometheus-compatible datasource rules is implemented in:\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/api/api_convert_prometheus.go`: `ConvertPrometheusSrv` service handles conversion of datasource-managed rules (from Prometheus, Loki, Mimir) to Grafana-managed alert rules. This includes:\n  - `RouteConvertPrometheusPostRuleGroup()`: Import rule groups from Prometheus-compatible sources\n  - `RouteConvertPrometheusGetRules()`: Retrieve imported rules\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/api/prometheus_conversion.go`: Handler for Prometheus rule conversion endpoints\n\n**Legacy Storage Adapters:**\nThe system provides legacy storage adapters to maintain backward compatibility:\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/legacy_storage.go`: Adapter for alert rules using the legacy API\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/recordingrule/legacy_storage.go`: Adapter for recording rules\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/notifications/routingtree/legacy_storage.go`: Adapter for notification routing policies\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/compat.go`: Compatibility conversion functions for alert rules and notification settings\n\n**Migration Infrastructure:**\nDatabase migrations for the unified alerting schema are in `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/`:\n- `migration_service_migration.go`: Moves legacy alert migration status from migration log to kvstore\n- `rule_notification_settings_mig.go`: Adds notification_settings columns to alert_rule and alert_rule_version tables\n- `tables.go`: Defines the schema for unified alerting tables\n\n**Integration Points:**\n- `pkg/services/provisioning/provisioning.go`: `ProvisionAlerting()` function orchestrates the provisioning of alert rules, contact points, notification policies, and mute timings\n- Feature flags like `alertingMigrationUI` enable the UI for migrating datasource-managed rules\n- Migration labels (prefixed with `__legacy_`) are added to rules during legacy migration to maintain routing compatibility\n\nThe architecture ensures that legacy alert rules can be progressively migrated to the unified alerting system while maintaining backward compatibility through the legacy storage layer and conversion functions."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Legacy Alerting to Unified Alerting Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement both the legacy alerting engine and the unified alerting (ngalert) engine: the legacy alert rule storage, the unified alert rule storage, and the migration script that converts legacy rules to unified format.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Go source files related to the legacy alerting to unified alerting migration in the grafana/grafana repository. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for relevant files.
Now let me search for files related to legacy alerting and unified alerting in the grafana repository:
Now let me create the comprehensive answer file with all the findings:

Perfect! I've created a comprehensive `answer.json` file documenting all Go source files in the grafana/grafana repository that implement the legacy alerting to unified alerting (ngalert) migration system.

## Summary of Findings

The answer includes:

### **Key Files Found (29 files)**
1. **Legacy Alerting Storage** - 4 files in `pkg/services/ngalert/notifier/legacy_storage/` for managing legacy Alertmanager configurations and notification channels
2. **Unified Alerting Core** - Core engine files including `ngalert.go`, alert rule models, and database stores
3. **Alert Rule Storage** - Database implementation and compatibility layers for persisting rules
4. **Provisioning Services** - Alert rule, contact point, and notification policy provisioning
5. **Migration APIs** - `api_convert_prometheus.go` for converting datasource-managed rules to Grafana-managed rules
6. **Legacy Adapters** - Compatibility layers in the API server for legacy storage
7. **Database Migrations** - Schema migrations in `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/`

### **Key Symbols (12 symbols)**
Including `AlertNG`, `AlertRule`, `DBstore`, `ConvertPrometheusSrv`, and migration-related constants

### **Migration Chain (5 functions)**
Showing how the system creates new alert rules through the provisioning service and migration endpoints

The answer document provides a detailed narrative explaining how the legacy alerting system is integrated with the new unified alerting engine, how rules are migrated from datasource-managed (Prometheus, Loki, Mimir) to Grafana-managed format, and how the system maintains backward compatibility through the legacy storage layer.

`MigratedUseLegacyChannelsLabel`)\n- `store/database.go`: Implements `DBstore` for persisting alert rules and instances to the database\n- `store/alert_rule.go`: Implements the `RuleStore` interface for CRUD operations on alert rules\n- `store/compat.go`: Contains conversion logic between different alert rule formats and notification settings\n\n**Alert Rule Storage Implementations:**\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/store/provisioning_store.go`: Implements the `ProvisioningStore` interface for managing rule provenance\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go`: Full database operations for alert rules including insertion, updates, and queries\n\n**Provisioning Service:**\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/alert_rules.go`: `AlertRuleService` handles rule provisioning, validation, and management\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/persist.go`: Defines `RuleStore` and `ProvisioningStore` interfaces\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/provisioning/contactpoints.go`: Manages contact points which are provisioned notification destinations\n\n**Migration from Datasource-Managed to Grafana-Managed Rules:**\nThe conversion API for Prometheus-compatible datasource rules is implemented in:\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/api/api_convert_prometheus.go`: `ConvertPrometheusSrv` service handles conversion of datasource-managed rules (from Prometheus, Loki, Mimir) to Grafana-managed alert rules. This includes:\n  - `RouteConvertPrometheusPostRuleGroup()`: Import rule groups from Prometheus-compatible sources\n  - `RouteConvertPrometheusGetRules()`: Retrieve imported rules\n- `pkg/services/ngalert/api/prometheus_conversion.go`: Handler for Prometheus rule conversion endpoints\n\n**Legacy Storage Adapters:**\nThe system provides legacy storage adapters to maintain backward compatibility:\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/legacy_storage.go`: Adapter for alert rules using the legacy API\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/recordingrule/legacy_storage.go`: Adapter for recording rules\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/notifications/routingtree/legacy_storage.go`: Adapter for notification routing policies\n- `pkg/registry/apps/alerting/rules/alertrule/compat.go`: Compatibility conversion functions for alert rules and notification settings\n\n**Migration Infrastructure:**\nDatabase migrations for the unified alerting schema are in `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/`:\n- `migration_service_migration.go`: Moves legacy alert migration status from migration log to kvstore\n- `rule_notification_settings_mig.go`: Adds notification_settings columns to alert_rule and alert_rule_version tables\n- `tables.go`: Defines the schema for unified alerting tables\n\n**Integration Points:**\n- `pkg/services/provisioning/provisioning.go`: `ProvisionAlerting()` function orchestrates the provisioning of alert rules, contact points, notification policies, and mute timings\n- Feature flags like `alertingMigrationUI` enable the UI for migrating datasource-managed rules\n- Migration labels (prefixed with `__legacy_`) are added to rules during legacy migration to maintain routing compatibility\n\nThe architecture ensures that legacy alert rules can be progressively migrated to the unified alerting system while maintaining backward compatibility through the legacy storage layer and conversion functions."
}
